title: Analysis of glycosphingolipids from human plasma
author: David A Priestman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.busvnwe6

steps.title: Use 50 µL plasma for GSL quantification
steps.instruction: Use 50 µL plasma for GSL quantification.

steps.title: Add 150 µL de-ionised water to...
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL de-ionised water to make the volume up to 200 µL in a 1.5 ml screw-cap tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.8 mL of chloroform/methanol (1:2, v/v) to give (C/M/W 4:8:3 final). Chloroform and methanol are very toxic, and methanol is flammable. Refer to the Safety Data Sheets.

steps.title: Leave Overnight at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Leave Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Vortex
steps.instruction: Vortex.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000 x g for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000 x g for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Transfer supernatant (about 1 mL )...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant (about 1 mL ) to new tube and separate into two phases: by adding 0.2 mL PBS and then 0.2 mL chloroform.

steps.title: Vortex
steps.instruction: Vortex.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000 x g for 00:10:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000 x g for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Remove very carefully the lower phase...
steps.instruction: Remove very carefully the lower phase to a new tube and retain the upper phase.

steps.title: Dry down the lower phase under...
steps.instruction: Dry down the lower phase under a stream of (oxygen-free) nitrogen in heating block ( 42 °C ).

steps.title: When dry, re-suspend the lower phase...
steps.instruction: When dry, re-suspend the lower phase in 20 µL chloroform/methanol (1:3).

steps.title: Add upper phase to lower phase and vortex
steps.instruction: Add upper phase to lower phase and vortex.

steps.title: Pre-equilibrate C18 columns (telos, Kinesis, UK)...
steps.instruction: Pre-equilibrate C18 columns (telos, Kinesis, UK) with 4 x 1 mL methanol and 3 x 1 mL deionised water.

steps.title: Load lower/upper phase mix onto column,...
steps.instruction: Load lower/upper phase mix onto column, let drip through gravity flow.

steps.title: Rinse sample tube with 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Rinse sample tube with 1 mL water, apply to column to wash.

steps.title: Wash column with 3 x 1 mL water
steps.instruction: Wash column with 3 x 1 mL water.

steps.title: Elute GSLs into a new tube with:
steps.instruction: Elute GSLs into a new tube with:

steps.title: 2 mL chloroform/methanol (98:2)
steps.instruction: 2 mL chloroform/methanol (98:2). Push through first 0.5 mL . You can use syringe with adapter that fits into top of column.

steps.title: 2 mL chloroform/methanol (1:3)
steps.instruction: 2 mL chloroform/methanol (1:3).

steps.title: 1 mL methanol
steps.instruction: 1 mL methanol.

steps.title: Vortex and leave Overnight at 4...
steps.instruction: Vortex and leave Overnight at 4 °C or carry on to enzymatic digestion.

steps.title: Vortex ( 5 mL , C18)...
steps.instruction: Vortex ( 5 mL , C18) and dry down samples under a stream of nitrogen in heating block ( 42 °C ).

steps.title: When about 150 µL sample remaining, transfer to 1
steps.instruction: When about 150 µL sample remaining, transfer to 1.5 mL screw-cap tube.

steps.title: Rinse sample tube with 200 µL...
steps.instruction: Rinse sample tube with 200 µL C:M 2:1, vortex and combine with the rest of the sample in the screw-cap tube.

steps.title: Dry down, under a slow stream...
steps.instruction: Dry down, under a slow stream of nitrogen in heating block ( 42 °C ).

steps.title: Re-suspend in 50 µL C:M 2:1,...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend in 50 µL C:M 2:1, vortex, dry down under a very slow stream of nitrogen. NB It is essential that ALL the sample is dried in the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Add 90 µL enzyme/buffer to each sample and vortex:
steps.instruction: Add 90 µL enzyme/buffer to each sample and vortex:

steps.title: rEGCase
steps.instruction: rEGCase : 4 µL enzyme (stored in freezer) plus 86 µL buffer per sample.

steps.title: rEGCase buffer (stored at 4 °C )
steps.instruction: rEGCase buffer (stored at 4 °C ): 0.6 % Triton in 50 millimolar (mM) sodium acetate pH 5.2.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 16:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 16:00:00 . rEGCaseI: recombinant Endoglycoceramidase I was custom synthesised by GenScript.

steps.title: Labelling mix
steps.instruction: Labelling mix : 30 mg/mL 2AA in labelling buffer and 45 mg/mL sodium cyanoborohydride Labelling buffer : 4% sodium acetate and 2% boric acid in methanol Dissolve 2AA in labelling buffer first with vortexing.

steps.title: Add 2AA in buffer to sodium cyanoborohydride and vortex
steps.instruction: Add 2AA in buffer to sodium cyanoborohydride and vortex. Sodium cyanoborohydride is very toxic. Refer to the Safety Data Sheet.

steps.title: Add 310 µL labelling mix to...
steps.instruction: Add 310 µL labelling mix to the 90 µL sample (digest) in 1.5 mL screw-cap tube.

steps.title: Incubate in oven at 80 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate in oven at 80 °C for 01:00:00 , vortexing at 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Allow to cool to Room temperature
steps.instruction: Allow to cool to Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 1 mL acetonitrile:water (97:3)
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL acetonitrile:water (97:3).

steps.title: Transfer from screw-cap tube to 15 mL tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer from screw-cap tube to 15 mL tubes.

steps.title: Rinse screw-cap tube with 2 x...
steps.instruction: Rinse screw-cap tube with 2 x 1 mL acetonitrile:water (97:3) and add to 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Pre-equilibrate 50 mg Discovery® DPA-6S SPE...
steps.instruction: Pre-equilibrate 50 mg Discovery® DPA-6S SPE Tube (supplied by Sigma-Aldrich) with: 1 mL acetonitrile 2 x 1 mL water 2 x 1 mL acetonitrile

steps.title: Apply samples ( 3
steps.instruction: Apply samples ( 3.4 mL ) to equilibrated DPA-6S columns, let drip through gravity flow.

steps.title: Wash columns with acetonitrile:water (95:5) →...
steps.instruction: Wash columns with acetonitrile:water (95:5) → add 1 mL into the 15 mL tubes to wash and add to columns, and then add 3 x 1 mL acetonitrile:water (95:5) directly onto the columns.

steps.title: Elute with 600 µL water into new tubes
steps.instruction: Elute with 600 µL water into new tubes.

steps.title: Add 60 µL
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL : 140 µL (sample:acetonitrile) to HPLC vials and vortex.

steps.title: Purified 2AA-labeled oligosaccharides are separated and...
steps.instruction: Purified 2AA-labeled oligosaccharides are separated and quantified by normal-phase high-performance liquid chromatography (NP-HPLC) as previously described (Neville et al. , 2004, see the reference below). Analysis of fluorescently labeled glycosphingolipid-derived oligosaccharides following ceramide glycanase digestion and anthranilic acid labeling DCA Neville, V Coquard, DA Priestman, DJM te Vruchte, DJ Sillence, Raymond A Dwek, Frances M Platt, Terry D Butters Analytical biochemistry 331 (2), 275-282, (2004). doi: 10.1016/j.ab.2004.03.051.

steps.title: The HPLC system consists of a...
steps.instruction: The HPLC system consists of a Waters Alliance 2695 separations module and an in-line Waters 2475 multi λ-fluorescence detector set at Ex λ360 nm and Em λ425 nm.

steps.title: The solid phase used is a 4
steps.instruction: The solid phase used is a 4.6 × 250 mm TSK gel-Amide 80 column maintained at 30 °C (Anachem, Luton, UK).

steps.title: The chromatographic flow rate is 0
steps.instruction: The chromatographic flow rate is 0.8 mL/min, and run time was 60 min. The total run time is 01:00:00 . Gradient conditions for Normal Phase HPLC. All chromatography was controlled and data were collected and processed using Waters Empower software.

steps.title: The mobile phases are acetonitrile (solvent...
steps.instruction: The mobile phases are acetonitrile (solvent A), de-ionised water (solvent B) and 100 millimolar (mM) ammonium acetate, pH 3.85 (solvent C).

steps.title: Starting conditions are 71
steps.instruction: Starting conditions are 71.6% solvent A. 8.4% solvent B and 20% solvent C at 0.8 mL/min constant flow-rate for 6 mins. 00:06:00

steps.title: The gradient is developed from 6...
steps.instruction: The gradient is developed from 6 min to 35 min by increasing solvent B from 8.4% to 27.2% with concomitant decrease in solvent A from 71.6% to 52.8 %. 00:29:00

steps.title: From 35 to 37 min solvent...
steps.instruction: From 35 to 37 min solvent B is increased to 57% and solvent A reduced to 23%, then maintained for 2 min up until 39 min. 00:04:00

steps.title: DPA-6S eluate is stable stored at -20 °C
steps.instruction: DPA-6S eluate is stable stored at -20 °C .

steps.title: For HPLC, inject 5 µL - 50 µL
steps.instruction: For HPLC, inject 5 µL - 50 µL .

steps.title: At 42 mins the flow-rate is increased to 1
steps.instruction: At 42 mins the flow-rate is increased to 1.2 mL/min and maintained for 12 min up to 54 min. 00:12:00

steps.title: Between 54 and 60 min the...
steps.instruction: Between 54 and 60 min the flow-rate is gradually returned to 0.8 mL/min. 00:06:00

steps.title: Individual GSL species are identified by...
steps.instruction: Individual GSL species are identified by their glucose unit values (GUs), calculated with the HPLC Empower software using a homopolymer dextran ladder (Fig. 2).

steps.title: Between 39 and 41 min the...
steps.instruction: Between 39 and 41 min the solvent ratios are returned to the starting conditions and then maintained for 1 min. 00:03:00

steps.title: Figure 1 GSL Biosynthetic pathway in...
steps.instruction: Figure 1 GSL Biosynthetic pathway in the background together with a typical HPLC trace below. GSLs detected in the profile are highlighted in the scheme. Figure 2 HPLC profiles for GSL standards and 2AA-labelled homopolymer dextran ladder. Glucose Units were calculated using the retention times of a 2AA-labelled homopolymer dextran ladder and are shown for each of the GSL standard peaks. Figure 3 GSL biosynthesis. Biosynthetic enzymes genes are indicated in the blue grid. Ganglioside names are abbreviated according to Svennerholm [1]. [1] L. Svennerholm, Designation and schematic structure of gangliosides and allied glycosphingolipids, Prog Brain Res 101 (1994) XI-XIV. Figure 4 'Typical HPLC profile for 2AA-labelled glycans released from human plasma glycosphingolipids with sugar structures indicated for each peak. In order to calculate values for total GSL concentrations, use the sum of the areas for all peaks from LacCer to GT1b. GlcCer, which elutes before LacCer, is not included because it is not fully hydrolysed by EGC’ase I. Figures created with BioRender.com

steps.title: To help identify GSL peaks in...
steps.instruction: To help identify GSL peaks in the plasma samples, compare the GUs of the peaks in the plasma samples with those in a GSL standard mix.
1.0 mg/ml 100 µL Matreya Ganglioside mix Cat No 1510 (Lac-Cer, GM3, GD
0.5 mg/ml 100 µL Matreya Ganglioside mix Cat No 1511 (GA1, GM1a, GD1a, GD1b,GT1b) 0.5 mg/ml 30 µL Matreya GM2 Ganglioside Cat No 1502 (GM
0.5 mg/ml 30 µL Sigma Asialo-GM2 Cat No G9398 (GA

steps.title: Dry down the mixture under nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Dry down the mixture under nitrogen and then digest, label and clean up as done for the plasma samples in steps 25-41.

steps.title: Aliquot and store at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Aliquot and store at -20 °C .

steps.title: In order to calculate molar quantities...
steps.instruction: In order to calculate molar quantities from integrated peaks in the chromatogram, inject a calibration standard containing 2.5 pmol 2AA-labelled chitotriose (Ludger) with each sample set (not shown).
